channel.title: Vector on Eric Windsor
channel.description: Recent content in Vector on Eric Windsor

item.title: Vector
item.description: Defination of Vector A vector is a directed line segment. Given points $P$ and $Q$ (either in the plane or in space), we denote with $\vec {PQ}$ the vector from $P$ to $Q$. The point $P$ is said to be the initial point of the vector, and the point $Q$ is the terminal point. The magnitude, length or norm of $\vec {PQ}$ is the length of the line segment $\overline {PQ}$: $|| \overline{PQ} ||$ = $||\vec {PQ} ||$ .
